title: Mapping External Claims to Roles
description:
## Granting a Role from an Incoming Claim

Users who log in through an **external OIDC Identity Provider** arrive with a claim `org_type=partner`. You want every such user to **automatically receive the Keycloak `partner` role** as they are brokered in.

Which kind of mapper do you configure?

options.text: An **Identity Provider mapper** (e.g. 'Claim to Role' / advanced claim-to-role) on that IdP, mapping `org_type=partner` to the `partner` role
A **protocol mapper** on the client, since protocol mappers translate incoming IdP claims into roles
No mapper — manually assign the `partner` role to each brokered user after they appear
A **client scope** mapper, which reads claims from the upstream IdP and assigns roles

explanation: Mapping an **incoming** identity (a claim from an external IdP, or an LDAP attribute/group) into a Keycloak attribute or role is the job of an **Identity Provider mapper** (or LDAP mapper for federation). A 'Claim to Role' / advanced claim-to-role IdP mapper assigns the `partner` role whenever the incoming token carries `org_type=partner`. (a) is correct. (b) and (d) confuse direction: **protocol mappers** (and client-scope mappers) shape the **outgoing** tokens Keycloak issues to *your* clients — they do not read or transform an upstream IdP's incoming claims. (c) is the manual approach the mapper exists to automate.

hints.title: Incoming vs outgoing
hints.body: IdP/LDAP mappers transform identities coming *into* Keycloak; protocol mappers shape tokens going *out* to your applications. The claim is arriving from an external IdP, so you need an IdP mapper.
